default.name: Config file with API key
default.context: Store API key in ~/.config/your-tool/config.json. Simple, standard CLI pattern.
default.when: CLI tools that connect to an API

default.name: OAuth2 device flow
default.context: User visits a URL and enters a code. Good for CLIs that need user identity without a browser redirect.
default.when: Enterprise CLIs, tools that need user identity

default.name: No auth needed
default.context: Local-only tool that processes files or data without connecting to a service.

typescript.name: npm (global install)
typescript.context: npm install -g your-tool. Standard for Node.js CLI tools. Easy to publish and update.

rust.name: cargo install + GitHub Releases
rust.context: Publish to crates.io for cargo install. Also provide pre-built binaries on GitHub Releases.

go.name: go install + GitHub Releases
go.context: go install for Go users. Pre-built binaries on GitHub Releases for everyone else.

python.name: pip install (PyPI)
python.context: Standard Python distribution. pip install your-tool. Easy to publish with twine.

default.name: Language package manager + GitHub Releases
default.context: Publish to your language's package registry and provide pre-built binaries for users without the runtime.

default.name: Pure CLI (flags and arguments)
default.context: Standard Unix-style CLI. Parseable output, scriptable, pipeable.

default.name: Interactive TUI (terminal UI)
default.context: Rich terminal interface with menus, tables, progress bars. Libraries: Ink (TS), Ratatui (Rust), Rich (Python).
default.when: User-facing tools where visual feedback helps

default.name: SQLite (local file)
default.context: Embedded database, no server. Perfect for CLI tools that need persistent local storage.

default.name: JSON/YAML config files
default.context: Store configuration and small data in ~/.config/your-tool/. No database dependency.

default.name: Cloud API (Supabase, Firebase)
default.context: For CLI tools that sync data to the cloud.
